memoryArtifacts.headline: Isleham St Andrew Church inside, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England
Elizabeth {Rich} Peyton
memoryArtifacts.description:
Seventh daughter of Richard Rich, Lord Rich of Leighs Priory Essex and Elizabeth Jenks, daughter of William, a London grocer.

Wife of Sir Robert Peyton, son of Sir Robert Peyton and Frances Hasilden. …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth Rich於大約1530年出生在英國倫敦時，她的父親Sir Richard Rich31歲，母親Elisabeth Jenkes28歲。她於大約1554年與Philip Craddock在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根傑利頓結婚。她去世於1591年10月17日在英國坎布利其爾易斯勒哈姆享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾易亞斯特 坎布利其爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth Rich于大约1530年出生在英国倫敦时，她的父亲Sir Richard Rich31岁，母亲Elisabeth Jenkes28岁。她于大约1554年与Philip Craddock在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根傑利頓结婚。她去世于1591年10月17日在英国坎布利其爾易斯勒哈姆享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰坎布利其爾易亞斯特 坎布利其爾。

nameMeanings.name: Rich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a wealthy man (or perhaps in some cases an ironic nickname for a pauper), from Middle English, Old French riche ‘rich, wealthy’, a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to ancient Germanic rīc ‘power(ful)’.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rich, a pet form of any of the post-Conquest names beginning in Rich-, such as Richer and especially Richard . Compare Rick .

English: either a topographic name from Middle English riche(Old English ric) ‘stream’, signifying one who lived beside a stream, as at Glynde Reach (Sussex), or perhaps a habitational name from the (now lost) village of Riche (Lincolnshire).

variants.name: Ritchie
meanings: Scottish: from a Scottish and northern English pet form of the personal name   Rich  , a short form of   Richard  . This surname is also common in Ulster.

variants.name: Ritch
meanings: English and Scottish (Orkney): either a variant of Rich , or perhaps from a short form of Ritchie , itself a diminutive based on Rich.

Altered form of German Ritsch, probably from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on rīc ‘power(ful)’ or hrōd ‘fame, renown’; or an altered form of Swiss German Rütsch, Ruetsch, from Alemannic short forms of Rudolf .

variants.name: Riches
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): variant of Rich , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Richer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Armand, Fernand, Herve, Laurent, Marcel, Aurele, Donat, Elphege, Emile, Flore, Gilles.

English (East Anglia), French, and German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hari, heri ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rich(i)er, Ricier, but was largely absorbed by the much more common Richard .

Altered form of French Héricher: from the ancient Germanic personal name Harigari, composed of the elements hari ‘army’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’, or a variant of Hérissé, a nickname for someone with spiky hair, from the adjective hérissé, a derivative of the verb se hérisser ‘to stand on end’.

variants.name: Riche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Ghislaine, Philippe, Yanick, Yvon.
French: nickname for a rich man or perhaps an ironical name for a pauper, from Old French riche ‘rich’. Compare Leriche .

variants.name: Richins
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ricun, a diminutive of Rich , itself a pet form of Richard , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. It appears that a dialectally different (Norman or Picard) form of the French name, with medial /-k-/, also survived in England for a time alongside forms with original /-tʃ-/.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth Rich was born about 1530, in London, England, her father, Sir Richard Rich, was 31 and her mother, Elisabeth Jenkes, was 28. She married Philip Craddock about 1554, in Cheriton, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. She died on 17 October 1591, in Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in East Cambridgeshire, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth Rich est née environ 1530, à Londres, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Rich, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth Jenkes, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Philip Craddock environ 1554, à Cheriton, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée le 17 octobre 1591, à Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à East Cambridgeshire, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Richが約1530年にイギリスロンドンで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Richは31歳，母Elisabeth Jenkesは28歳であった。約1554年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンチェリトンでPhilip Craddockと結婚した。彼女は1591年10月17日にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアイスレハムで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアエアスト カンブリジェシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Rich(이)가 대략 1530년 영국 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Richard Rich(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth Jenkes(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 대략 1554년 영국 웨일스 글램르건 처리턴에서 Philip Craddock(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1591년 10월 17일 영국 캄브리지실르 이슬레함에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 이스트 캄브리지실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth Rich родилась около 1530 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Richard Rich было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth Jenkes, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip Craddock около 1554 г. в местности чиритон, глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. Она умерла 17 октября 1591 г. в местности ислэам, камбриджешир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности эаст камбриджешир, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания.
